heroTagline: The light is on, the roots run deep.
missionText: We started with one encyclopedia about a small London neighborhood. Then padel. Then mushrooms in Portugal. Then a whole city. Seven projects later, we understood: this isn't about topics. It's about building knowledge tools that belong to the people who use them. No investors, no algorithms, no paywalls. The lantern burns, the roots hold.
missionPrinciple1Text: Every article, every translation, every update. No subscriptions, no premium tiers, no 'sign up to continue reading'. The whole library, front door open.
missionPrinciple2Text: We'd rather have 50 solid articles than 500 thin ones. Each piece gets checked, sourced, and written in a way that respects your time.
missionPrinciple3Text: Five people, zero investors. We write because the subject matters to us — not because someone's paying for clicks.
barnesDesc: A quiet London neighborhood with 800 years of stories. Local pubs, Victorian streets, the duck pond. We're writing them down before they're forgotten.
padelDesc: The fastest-growing racket sport in the world. Rules, technique, courts, gear, tournaments — all in one place, in three languages.
setubalDesc: A Portuguese city where the mountains meet the sea. Markets, routes, restaurants, history — written by people who actually walk the streets.
cogumelosDesc: Portuguese mushrooms. Which ones to eat, which ones to photograph from a safe distance. Every species verified, every warning real.
tarotDesc: Tarot as history, art, and cultural artifact. Decks, symbolism, schools of thought. Not fortune-telling — understanding.
frameworkVerifyText: Every article has a status: verified, partially verified, or unverified. Sources listed. Confidence rated. No guessing.
frameworkI18nText: Two to three languages per encyclopedia. Not machine translation — real editorial work in each language.
frameworkSafetyText: Mushroom toxicity warnings. Medication interactions. Allergen alerts. When content can hurt, we mark it clearly.
frameworkDeployText: Push to publish. Branded emails on new releases. Status monitoring. The boring stuff that keeps a library running.
frameworkComplianceText: GDPR, security headers, WCAG accessibility, cookie consent. Not because we have to — because the library should be safe for everyone who walks in.
teamSubtitle: The fornits who keep the light on
aliceOneliner: Jumps down the rabbit hole with a lantern. Always comes back with something.
gonzoOneliner: Goes into the Zone for artifacts. Verifies everything twice, trusts nothing once.
cooperOneliner: Sees patterns where others see noise. The owls are not what they seem.
lanternOneliner: You don't see him, but without him it's dark. Tends the light, keeps the warmth.
levOneliner: Walls and locks of the library. Every door open, every lock in place.
roadmapDoneText: 7 encyclopedias. 1,500+ articles across 3 languages. A text-based quest. Full GDPR compliance. Safety protocols for hazardous content.
roadmapNowText: Deepening existing encyclopedias. A comic based on the quest. New translations and cross-references between projects.
roadmapNextText: New encyclopedias built to the same standards. Expanding the ecosystem with new topics and languages.
roadmapFutureText: We'll see. The lantern burns, the roots hold — the rest will come.
supportText: Everything here is free. Always will be. No ads, no subscriptions, no tricks. But someone keeps the light on. If you want to help — the door is open.
supportHow: Your support keeps servers running, fonts licensed, articles checked, and mushroom warnings accurate.
supportFormulas: One coffee = one article stays free.
Free knowledge costs someone. Today it can cost a coffee.
Five people, no investors, zero ads. But the light stays on.
footerEndorsement: FolkUp is an independent project. Not affiliated with any organization.
dedication: To my sons, whom I taught to play, with whom I played, and who teach me to play again
